entries.headline: Your Numbers\nAre Lying to You.
entries.body: Most operators trust their POS. BarLedger cross-references actual stock against theoretical usage and shows you exactly where the gap lives.
entries.cta: Book a free demo. Link in bio.

entries.headline: Your Whole Bar.\nCounted in Minutes.
entries.body: No clipboards, no spreadsheets, no guesswork. Pull up BarLedger on any mobile device and scan your stock room to shelf in a fraction of the time.
entries.cta: See it in action. Link in bio.

entries.headline: Build It Right.\nPrice It Right.
entries.body: BarLedger's recipe builder ties every ingredient to your live inventory cost. Know your pour cost per cocktail before it ever hits the menu.
entries.cta: Try it free for 14 days. Link in bio.

entries.headline: Stop Discovering\nProblems at Month End.
entries.body: BarLedger generates automated PDF variance reports so ownership sees the bleed in real time, not six weeks after the damage is done.
entries.cta: Your first 14 days are free. Link in bio.

entries.headline: A Quarter Ounce\nOver Costs Thousands.
entries.body: A bartender overpouring by just 0.25oz per drink can erase 4 to 6% of your gross spirit revenue annually. BarLedger makes that variance impossible to ignore.
entries.cta: Know your numbers. Link in bio.

entries.headline: You're Not Getting\nWhat You Paid For.
entries.body: A standard half-barrel yields 124 pints, but foam loss, line waste, and overpours routinely cut that by 15%. BarLedger tracks your draft yields pour by pour.
entries.cta: Stop losing kegs. Link in bio.

entries.headline: Four Glasses Per\nBottle Is a Myth.
entries.body: Industry standard is 5 pours per 750ml at 5oz. If your team is pouring 6oz, you're giving away a full glass per bottle. BarLedger catches it before it compounds.
entries.cta: Lock in your pour standard. Link in bio.

entries.headline: Pretty Bottles.\nHidden Losses.
entries.body: Your back bar looks great to guests and terrible to your bottom line if nobody is tracking it. BarLedger puts a number on every bottle so nothing walks out unaccounted for.
entries.cta: Get control of your bar. Link in bio.

entries.headline: Your Prices Held.Your Margins Didn't.
entries.body: Supplier costs shift constantly, but most operators update recipe costs once a year at best. BarLedger ties every recipe to live ingredient pricing so your pour cost reflects today, not last January.
entries.cta: Start your free 14-day trial. Link in bio.

entries.headline: Every Bottle Accounted For. \nEvery Dollar Protected.
entries.body: BarLedger connects what you receive, what you use, and what you sell into one clean picture. Operators who run tight know exactly where their inventory stands at any moment.
entries.cta: Run a tighter bar. Link in bio.

entries.headline: Tight Operations\nDon't Happen by Accident.
entries.body: The most profitable bars run on systems, not instincts. BarLedger gives you a live, accurate count of every item so the decisions you make are based on real numbers, not gut feel.
entries.cta: Run a tighter bar. Link in bio.

entries.headline: Buying on Habit\nCosts More Than You Think.
entries.body: Without accurate on-hand counts, most bars over-order by 10 to 20% every week, tying up cash in excess stock that sits, shifts, and sometimes disappears. BarLedger tells you exactly what you need before you ever open a purchase order.
entries.cta: Order smarter starting today. Link in bio.

entries.headline: What You Ordered \nand \n What You Got Are Not the Same.
entries.body: Short pours, missing bottles, and invoice errors at receiving can quietly strip 3 to 5% from your margins before a single drink is poured. BarLedger ties every delivery to your purchase order so discrepancies surface immediately.
entries.cta: Stop paying for product you never got. Link in bio.

entries.headline: The Shrink Isn't Random.\nIt Has a Pattern.
entries.body: Bars that count manually miss the same gaps every cycle. BarLedger surfaces recurring variances by category, location, and period so you can see exactly where the pattern lives and stop it.
entries.cta: Find your pattern before it costs more. Link in bio.

entries.headline: You Built the Menu.\nDid You Build the Margin?
entries.body: Most bar menus are priced on feel, not math. BarLedger calculates the true cost of every recipe down to the pour so you know exactly what each drink costs before it hits the floor.
entries.cta: Build menus that actually make money. Link in bio.
